10.response: {"Q0":"I was afraid the astronaut would come back in the next round so I was considering to keep digging in the blue planet in the next round but my instinct was that it didn't come back, and I was right."}

13.response: {"Q0":"Everything was great and the animations were also very good"}

0.response: {"Q0":"For the galaxies there were some obvious answers. For the less obvious I tried to do the math of percentage times the value to see which was greater because I feel that gives a mathematical edge for example (50% for 4$ would be 0.5*4=2 against 100% for 1$ would be obviously 1, so I would choose 4$). Of course some were difficult and I didn't have time to choose the better option and just went by feeling of which was better. For the planets it was easier. Always dig the blue and red planets IF there were astronauts. I felt that it was always worth to try finding a gem because it gives 20 gold and only uses 3 to try. Also the odds seemed very decent to find a gem, I would have to dig a rock 7 times in a row for it to not be worth and that never happened. For the meteor shower I decided to play safe. Obviously collecting all the gems for which there was an astronaut because each gives a surplus of 10 gold (minus 10 plus 20) and decided not to risk on the ice because I didn't know the gem/rock odds."}

7.response: {"Q0":"It didn't impact my decision, because I didn't think it changed the gem/rock odds."}

9.response: {"Q0":"Selecting galaxies. I felt that 3 seconds were very easy to identify the color of the planet and dig, so I was comfortable in that time. For the galaxies I was very focused in the initial 1.8 seconds in order to make my decision and then answer instantly in the 1 second I had."}

1.response: {"Q0":"No, I kept it throughout the game"}

8.response: {"Q0":"As I said before the probability times the value. The galaxy with the highest would be my prefered choice."}
